testPageVerification.note: Test page is a blog post (ID 181) at /blog/test-post-hero/, not a page slug — taktician_wp_count_blocks required resource:'posts' and id:181

rawContent.note: compare_raw_content does not support blog post slugs (only pages). Skipped — full QA run performed.

html.note: compare_html not run as a result. Full QA run performed.

contentComparison.reason: Content comparison unavailable for blog posts — ran full QA

bundledIntoScreenJs.detail: public/js/screen.js contains '.post-hero .top-gradient' selector. webpack.config.js entry 'js/screen' uses glob.sync('./blocks/**/!(*index).js') which automatically picks up PostHero.js. Confirmed by grep: 'document.querySelectorAll(".post-hero .top-gradient")' present in minified bundle.

selectorScoping.detail: PostHero.js line 6: document.querySelectorAll('.post-hero .top-gradient') — correctly scoped to .post-hero wrapper, not the generic .hero class used by Hero.js ('.hero .top-gradient').

resizeObserverCapsMaxHeight.detail: PostHero.js: forEach gradient -> closest('main') || document.querySelector('main') -> updateMaxHeight() sets gradient.style.maxHeight = main.offsetHeight + 'px' -> new ResizeObserver(updateMaxHeight).observe(main). Logic matches Hero.js pattern exactly. Rendered page confirms .top-gradient elements render inside <main> with ResizeObserver active (no JS errors reported by common.NO_JS_ERRORS check).

gradientHeight150vh.detail: Hero.css line 7: height: 150vh on .top-gradient. PostHero.php wrapper has class 'post-hero hero' (line 28), inheriting Hero.css styles via the shared 'hero' class. No PostHero.css needed — DEC-002 confirmed correct. No 150vw remnant found anywhere in codebase.

noJsErrors.detail: common.NO_JS_ERRORS automated check passed. Page loads cleanly at all viewports.

blockRendersCorrectly.detail: All 4 instances render correctly: topic pills, h1 title, background image with mask, author line, date line. page_audit confirmed .top-gradient, .hero-inner, .flex.flex-wrap (topic pills), h1, .mt-12 (author/date) all present in all 4 sections. 5 sections total because one demo-container header section is also counted.

build.detail: bun run build passes with 0 errors. 10 pre-existing react-hooks/exhaustive-deps warnings (2 in PostHero.tsx) — all pre-existing, none introduced by this edit.

lint.detail: 10 react-hooks/exhaustive-deps warnings across 7 files — all pre-existing, none in PostHero.js (the new file). PostHero.js is plain ES5 JS with no React hooks, so no lint issues apply to it.

registration.detail: block.json: apiVersion 3, name takt/post-hero, category takt-theme, textdomain takt. No viewScript registered in block.json — PostHero.js is bundled into the global screen.js via webpack glob, not registered as a viewScript. This is correct per DEC-003 (same pattern as Hero.js, QuickNavigation.js).

attributes.detail: No attribute changes in this edit. Existing attributes (anchor, showExcerpt, useTopicColor) unchanged. PHP docblock lists all three @var annotations. No $attributes['key'] anti-pattern found.

edgeCases.detail: PostHero.js guards against missing <main> element: 'if (!main) return'. No crash if .top-gradient elements are absent (forEach is a no-op). ResizeObserver correctly observes <main> not the gradient itself.

interactiveBehavior.reason: No interactive elements. The ResizeObserver runs automatically on DOMContentLoaded — no user interaction required.

accessibility.detail: No interactive elements added. Decorative background image uses alt='' role='presentation'. Topic pills are <span> elements (read-only labels). Heading hierarchy: single h1 per block instance. common.IMAGES_ALT automated check passed.
accessibility.patternComplianceReason: No WAI-ARIA interactive patterns — no tabs, accordion, carousel, modal, or disclosure elements.

markupDuplication.detail: No responsive markup duplication. Single DOM tree at all breakpoints. CSS handles responsive layout.

warnings.description: taktician_validate_all reports 'Root wrapper element mismatch: TSX uses <inspectorcontrols>, PHP uses <section>'. This is a false positive — InspectorControls is a Gutenberg editor sidebar component that renders outside the block DOM. The actual block wrapper in PostHero.tsx uses useBlockProps() on a <section>. Not a real sync issue.

tsxPhpSync.detail: PHP template uses no $attributes['key'] anti-pattern. @var docblock present for showExcerpt and useTopicColor. theme_block_props() used on wrapper. class_name() used for conditional accent color class.

specCompliance.detail: All 3 planning spec requirements verified: (1) PostHero.js created with ResizeObserver, (2) selector scoped to .post-hero .top-gradient, (3) bundled into screen.js via webpack glob. No CSS changes needed — confirmed Hero.css height: 150vh inherited via shared 'hero' class. All 4 test page instances render correctly inside takt/demo-container wrappers.

results.common.RESPONSIVE_RENDER: PASS (manual — screenshots at all 4 breakpoints confirm no layout breakage)
results.common.DARK_THEME: SKIP (block has no theme/color variant attribute)
results.common.REDUCED_MOTION: PASS (automated — no animations in block CSS)

visualObservations.375px: All 4 PostHero instances render cleanly at mobile. Title is large and readable. Topic pills stack/wrap correctly. Author and date lines visible below title. Background image displays with gradient mask. Gradient blends naturally into page background. No horizontal overflow. Page footer and Related section render below block instances.
visualObservations.768px: Tablet layout shows wider hero with more padding. Background image occupies right portion. Title, pills, author/date column on left. All 4 variants render correctly (default, excerpt, topic pills, full options). Color-accented variant shows green accent pill. Gradient transitions smoothly.
visualObservations.1024px: Laptop layout — hero spans full width. Title font larger relative to mobile. All content elements display correctly. The 'FULL OPTIONS: EXCERPT + TOPIC COLOR' variant shows the excerpt text below the title as expected. No layout issues.
visualObservations.1440px: Full desktop — large hero image with gradient fade. Post meta (author, date) clearly visible. All 4 variations display as expected. Gradient height is clearly contained within the <main> element area (ResizeObserver max-height working). No overflow or clipping issues.

passedChecks: PostHero.js bundled into public/js/screen.js via webpack glob pattern
ResizeObserver observes <main> and caps .top-gradient max-height dynamically
Null guard: if (!main) return prevents crash when <main> is absent
gradient height 150vh inherited from Hero.css via shared 'hero' class on wrapper
No JS errors on page load
Block renders correctly with all content: title, pills, image, author, date
showExcerpt variant renders excerpt text when true
useTopicColor variant applies accent color class when topic has accent_color meta
Full options variant (excerpt + topic color) renders correctly
No horizontal overflow at any breakpoint
PHP docblock present for all camelCase attributes
